mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns following a 0.2pp UNRATE jump are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or if the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling industrial production indicates a structural shift from manufacturing to services, creating sectoral rotation risks that depress broad equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power if the joint HOUST-INDPRO divergence fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed instances, or if single-series ICs dominate the interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation occurring while the federal funds rate remains stationary signals an impending policy tightening that will eventually contract liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered episodes, or if the CPI-FEDFUNDS interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product expands corporate revenue bases, directly supporting earnings per share growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 prints are not statistically different from zero, or if the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production below its historical trend level indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening business investment, which suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO breaches the 115.0 threshold, or if the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline momentum model.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in total nonfarm payrolls and consumer credit outstanding reflect a synchronized contraction in employment and household spending, which triggers a sharp earnings recession and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly period.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both decline for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails if 60-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, or if the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts peaking while consumer price inflation accelerates signals an overheating economy that will force aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST reaches a 12-month high while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns following the trigger are positive in more than 60% of historical instances, or if the combined proxy pair shows no directional consistency in walk-forward testing.
